hook.text: TLS protects billions of connections every day. What actually happens during that invisible handshake?

buildup.text: The client says hello and lists the encryption methods it supports. The server picks one and sends its certificate.

discovery.text: They agree on a shared secret using Diffie-Hellman or similar. From then on, all data is symmetrically encrypted.

twist.text: TLS 1.3 reduced the handshake to one round trip. Faster setup without compromising security.

climax.text: If anyone tampers with a TLS message, the integrity check fails and the connection drops immediately.

punchline.text: TLS is trust, speed, and secrecy in one handshake.

quiz.question: What improvement did TLS 1.3 bring?
options: Reduced the handshake to one round trip
